skill.name: grilling
skill.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design. Use when the user wants to stress-test a plan before building, or uses any 'grill' trigger phrases.
skill.summary: Stress-test plans and designs through focused, sequential questioning.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 6, including one duplicate report. Review found no cryptographic code, scripts, network access, or prompt injection attempt. The flagged text is ordinary planning language in a short instruction-only skill.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 3 contains the skill description for interviewing a user about a plan or design. No cryptographic operation, weak algorithm, executable code, or secret handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain frontmatter description text. The duplicate static reports at this line have no supporting security context.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 6 instructs the assistant to question a plan and resolve design decisions. It does not reference cryptography, hashing, encryption, credentials, or executable behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is natural language guidance for planning interviews. No evidence of a weak cryptographic algorithm or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss dependencies, tradeoffs, and unclear assumptions before work begins. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured planning interview.
actual_capabilities: Asks focused questions about a plan or design one at a time.
Recommends an answer for each question to speed decision making.
Explores codebase context when a question can be answered from existing files.
Helps surface dependencies between design decisions before implementation.
Supports planning discussions triggered by direct requests to stress-test an idea.
limitations: Does not execute code, run tests, or modify files by itself.
Depends on user feedback to continue multi-step questioning.
Cannot validate facts that are absent from the conversation or codebase.
May be too intensive for simple tasks that already have clear requirements.

use_cases.title: Review a Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Challenge scope, user flows, dependencies, and rollout details before a feature enters implementation.

use_cases.title: Pressure-Test Technical Design
use_cases.description: Identify missing decisions in architecture, data flow, migration, and failure handling.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Stakeholder Review
use_cases.description: Find unclear assumptions and likely objections before presenting a plan to decision makers.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Plan Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill this plan one question at a time. Recommend an answer for each question before waiting for my response.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a short plan and want quick scrutiny.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Design Interview
prompt_templates.prompt: Stress-test this feature design. Start with the highest-risk assumption, then continue only after my answer.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before building a user-facing feature.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Decision Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Interview me about this architecture proposal. Explore dependencies between decisions and inspect the codebase when useful.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when technical choices may affect multiple systems.

prompt_templates.title: Launch Readiness Challenge
prompt_templates.prompt: Question this launch plan until risks, owners, rollback steps, and unresolved decisions are clear.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before a release, migration, or operational change.

output_examples.input: Grill my plan to add team workspaces to our app.
output_examples.output: First question: Which existing account model owns workspace membership? Recommended answer: choose one owner model before designing invites or permissions.

output_examples.input: Stress-test this migration approach before I implement it.
output_examples.output: First question: What is the rollback path if the new schema causes production errors? Recommended answer: define a reversible migration first.

output_examples.input: Interview me about this API redesign.
output_examples.output: First question: Which clients must remain compatible during rollout? Recommended answer: list clients and version constraints before changing endpoints.

best_practices: Provide the plan, constraints, and known risks before starting the interview.
Answer each question directly so the next question can build on it.
Ask the assistant to inspect relevant files when repository context should guide the answer.
anti_patterns: Use it after implementation when you only need a final code review.
Ask for many questions at once when sequential decisions are required.
Treat recommended answers as final decisions without checking team constraints.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It runs a structured interview to expose unclear assumptions, dependencies, and risks in a plan or design.

faq.question: Does it change files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is instruction-only and does not include scripts or file modification steps.

faq.question: Can it inspect a codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the assistant to explore the codebase when existing files can answer a planning question.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Product managers, engineers, and team leads can use it before committing to a plan.

faq.question: How should I start?
faq.answer: Share the plan and ask the assistant to grill it one question at a time.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no scripts, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection content.
